element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational enemy encounters establish core combat rhythm
Breakable drums contain weapons; each character has preferred weapon with unique combos
New mobility mechanic enables tactical positioning and attack variety
Can grab from behind; adds grappling layer to combat toolkit
Fills during regular attacks; powerful single-use ability when full; auto-empties if not used within time window
Street Fighter-style input commands for special moves
Multiple routes through levels; changes encountered areas, enemies, and boss encounters
Resource management during ongoing combat pressure
Single-player option to have computer ally in two-player game structure
Each of six stages has unique boss; boss selection varies by branching path taken

typical_relief_triggers: Food item pickups restore health mid-stage
Weapon pickups provide temporary power advantage
Super Move execution creates moment of control
typical_tension_triggers: Weapon depletion returning to unarmed combat
Super gauge emptying automatically if not used
Path branching decisions create uncertainty about upcoming challenges
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sustained action with rhythmic peaks at boss encounters; branching paths introduce decision-tension separate from combat tension; Super gauge creates strategic micro-cycles of build-and-release within continuous combat

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Breakable drums contain weapons and health items; branching paths signal optional route choices
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Yashichi invincibility item hidden behind foreground objects (example: sewer stage in Round 5)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundation stage introduces all core mechanics (dash, grab, special moves, Super gauge) with manageable enemy pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Path branching introduces complexity; boss encounters begin featuring unique patterns; Super Move gauge timing becomes tactically relevant
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 2+ boss Callman appears in multiple locations due to branching; difficulty escalates through boss encounters and increased enemy density
notable_spikes: Stage 2 boss Callman encounter (varies by path taken)
Super gauge auto-empty timeout creates precision demands
Path-dependent boss selection creates variable difficulty peaks

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of six stages features multiple traversable paths that change encountered areas, enemy types, and boss encounters. Same boss may appear in different locations depending on route. Ending varies by character, path, and difficulty.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 2 (features Callman at multiple possible locations)

notable_level_patterns.description: Drums scattered throughout stages contain weapons (character-specific for unique combos), health food, and bonus items. Mechanical rhythm of destruction and collection provides pacing micro-cycles.
Each of six stages concludes with a unique boss character. Boss selection and difficulty can shift based on branching path taken during stage progression.
Super Move gauge builds during regular combat but auto-empties if held full for too long without activation. Creates strategic tension: hold for perfect moment vs. risk losing power entirely.
